# Journal — Usage Guide

*Mode: agent · 🤖 needs an LLM endpoint*

Builds GnuCash TDS journal entries from a 26AS workbook plus your GnuCash book. It reads Part I of the workbook, matches each deductor to one of your accounts (deterministic fuzzy match first, LLM fallback for the hard cases), and writes a balanced journal CSV ready to import.

## When to use it

Use this after "26AS Convert", when you want the TDS recorded in GnuCash as journal entries instead of typing each one by hand.

## Inputs

- **26AS workbook (Convert output)** (required) — accepts: Excel (.xlsx) matching *-26AS.xlsx.
  - The 26AS workbook produced by 26AS Convert — pick it from the list of prior outputs.
  - ⚠️ Run 26AS Convert first; the picker only shows workbooks from earlier runs.
- **GnuCash file** (required) — accepts: GnuCash file (.gnucash).
  - Your GnuCash book — used only to read account names for matching.
  - ⚠️ Close the book in GnuCash before running so the file is not locked. It is never modified.

## How to run

1. Pick the 26AS workbook from the dropdown of prior outputs.
2. Select your .gnucash book (make sure GnuCash has it closed).
3. Click Run; review any deductors flagged as unmatched.
4. Import the CSV into GnuCash: File > Import > Import Transactions from CSV.

## Output

Output is written to the **`Data/outputs/`** folder, named:

```
Data/outputs/YYYY-MM-DD-HHMMSS-<input>-tds-journals.csv
```

Files produced:

- **`YYYY-MM-DD-HHMMSS-<input>-tds-journals.csv`** — One balanced journal transaction per TDS entry (every transaction's amounts sum to zero), debiting the TDS receivable / asset account and crediting the income account, ready for GnuCash CSV import.

## Tips

Entries whose deductor could not be matched to an account are flagged so you can fix the account name in your book (or add the missing account) and re-run.

## Troubleshooting

| If… | Then… |
|------|-------|
| Run errors mentioning the endpoint / model. | This skill uses an LLM for fallback matching. Start Ollama (or set an endpoint in Settings). |
| A deductor was assigned the wrong account. | Rename/add the account in GnuCash so its name is closer to the deductor, then re-run. |
